THREE WAYS TO THINK.
ONE PURPOSE.

CAELYS brings three distinct intellectual verticals under one roof — each rigorous, each transformative, each yours to choose.

Model UN

Diplomacy & Global Policy

Explore

Debate & Rhetoric

Argumentation & Persuasion

Explore

Climate Conclave

Science, Policy & Action

Explore

> VERTICAL 01

MODEL UNITED NATIONS

Step into the chambers of global diplomacy. At CAELYS MUN, you don't just play a role — you inhabit one. Represent nations, navigate geopolitical tensions, and negotiate resolutions that reflect the complexity of the real world.

Committees are crafted with care — every agenda is chosen for its relevance, depth, and potential for genuine debate. Directors are trained to facilitate, not dominate. The room is yours to run.

Format

  • Two-day committee sessions
  • Western format with formal and informal caucuses
  • Position papers required for all delegates
  • Draft resolution writing and voting procedure

Committees

UNSC

UN Security Council

Redefining the Veto: Reforming UNSC in the Era of Multipolar Power

UNGA

UN General Assembly

AI Governance and Digital Sovereignty in Developing Nations

WHO

World Health Org

Pandemic Preparedness: Equitable Access to Vaccines in Low-Income Countries

ECOSOC

Economic & Social Council

Financing the SDGs: Innovative Mechanisms for Developing Economies

Best Delegate, Best Position Paper, High Commendation awarded

Register for MUN

> VERTICAL 02

DEBATE & RHETORIC

Words have power. CAELYS Debate is where you learn to wield them precisely. Built on the Asian Parliamentary (AP) format, the tournament demands logic, evidence, wit, and composure in equal measure.

Whether you come as a seasoned debater or a first-timer with fire in your gut, you will be challenged, coached, and transformed over two days of structured competition.

Tournament Structure

Day 1

  • AP format — teams of 3
  • Preliminary rounds (4 rounds)
  • Impromptu motion disclosure
  • Feedback after each round

Day 2

  • Quarter & semi-finals
  • Grand Final — open audience
  • Best Debater adjudication
  • Awards ceremony

AP requires teams of 3. Register as a team or as an individual — we will match solo participants into teams before the event.

Best Debater, Best Speaker, Best Team awarded

Register for Debate

> VERTICAL 03

CLIMATE CONCLAVE

The Climate Conclave is not a debate about whether the climate crisis is real. It is a room of people who know it is, and are working to figure out what to do next. Science, policy, technology, and community — four working groups, one shared urgency.

Unlike traditional MUN, the Conclave produces a tangible outcome — a policy brief, a community action plan, or a technology roadmap — presented publicly on Day 2.

Working Groups

Scientific

Evidence, data, and the science of climate change in the Indian context

Policy

Government frameworks, international agreements, and regulatory mechanisms

Technology

Clean energy, green innovation, and scalable climate tech solutions

Community

Grassroots action, social equity, and behaviour-led change

Best Working Group & Best Delegate recognised. Policy brief published post-event.

Register for Climate Conclave
